heading: Requirements with respect to administration of voter registration

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of registration with a motor vehicle application under , if the valid voter registration form of the applicant is submitted to the appropriate State motor vehicle authority not later than the lesser of 30 days, or the period provided by State law, before the date of the election;section 20504 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of registration by mail under , if the valid voter registration form of the applicant is postmarked not later than the lesser of 30 days, or the period provided by State law, before the date of the election;section 20505 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of registration at a voter registration agency, if the valid voter registration form of the applicant is accepted at the voter registration agency not later than the lesser of 30 days, or the period provided by State law, before the date of the election; and

children.label: D
children.text: in any other case, if the valid voter registration form of the applicant is received by the appropriate State election official not later than the lesser of 30 days, or the period provided by State law, before the date of the election;
require the appropriate State election official to send notice to each applicant of the disposition of the application;

children.label: A
children.text: at the request of the registrant;

children.label: B
children.text: as provided by State law, by reason of criminal conviction or mental incapacity; or

children.label: C
children.text: as provided under paragraph (4);

children.label: A
children.text: the death of the registrant; or

children.label: B
children.text: a change in the residence of the registrant, in accordance with subsections (b), (c), and (d);

children.label: A
children.text: voter eligibility requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: penalties provided by law for submission of a false voter registration application; and
ensure that the identity of the voter registration agency through which any particular voter is registered is not disclosed to the public.
shall be uniform, nondiscriminatory, and in compliance with the Voting Rights Act of 1965 ( et seq.) [now et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 197352 U.S.C. 10301

children.label: A
children.text: has not either notified the applicable registrar (in person or in writing) or responded during the period described in subparagraph (B) to the notice sent by the applicable registrar; and then

children.label: B
children.text: has not voted or appeared to vote in 2 or more consecutive general elections for Federal office.

children.label: A
children.text: change-of-address information supplied by the Postal Service through its licensees is used to identify registrants whose addresses may have changed; and

children.label: i
children.text: a registrant has moved to a different residence address in the same registrar’s jurisdiction in which the registrant is currently registered, the registrar changes the registration records to show the new address and sends the registrant a notice of the change by forwardable mail and a postage prepaid pre-addressed return form by which the registrant may verify or correct the address information; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the registrant has moved to a different residence address not in the same registrar’s jurisdiction, the registrar uses the notice procedure described in subsection (d)(2) to confirm the change of address.

children.label: A
children.text: A State shall complete, not later than 90 days prior to the date of a primary or general election for Federal office, any program the purpose of which is to systematically remove the names of ineligible voters from the official lists of eligible voters.

children.label: i
children.text: the removal of names from official lists of voters on a basis described in paragraph (3)(A) or (B) or (4)(A) of subsection (a); or

children.label: ii
children.text: correction of registration records pursuant to this chapter.

children.heading: Removal of names from voting rolls
children.label: d Removal of names from voting rolls
A
children.text: confirms in writing that the registrant has changed residence to a place outside the registrar’s jurisdiction in which the registrant is registered; or

children.label: i
children.text: has failed to respond to a notice described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: has not voted or appeared to vote (and, if necessary, correct the registrar’s record of the registrant’s address) in an election during the period beginning on the date of the notice and ending on the day after the date of the second general election for Federal office that occurs after the date of the notice.

children.label: A
children.text: If the registrant did not change his or her residence, or changed residence but remained in the registrar’s jurisdiction, the registrant should return the card not later than the time provided for mail registration under subsection (a)(1)(B). If the card is not returned, affirmation or confirmation of the registrant’s address may be required before the registrant is permitted to vote in a Federal election during the period beginning on the date of the notice and ending on the day after the date of the second general election for Federal office that occurs after the date of the notice, and if the registrant does not vote in an election during that period the registrant’s name will be removed from the list of eligible voters.

children.label: B
children.text: If the registrant has changed residence to a place outside the registrar’s jurisdiction in which the registrant is registered, information concerning how the registrant can continue to be eligible to vote.
A voting registrar shall correct an official list of eligible voters in elections for Federal office in accordance with change of residence information obtained in conformance with this subsection.

children.heading: Procedure for voting following failure to return card
children.label: e Procedure for voting following failure to return card

children.text: A registrant who has moved from an address in the area covered by a polling place to an address in the same area shall, notwithstanding failure to notify the registrar of the change of address prior to the date of an election, be permitted to vote at that polling place upon oral or written affirmation by the registrant of the change of address before an election official at that polling place.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be permitted to correct the voting records and vote at the registrant’s former polling place, upon oral or written affirmation by the registrant of the new address before an election official at that polling place; or

children.label: I
children.text: shall be permitted to correct the voting records and vote at a central location within the same registrar’s jurisdiction designated by the registrar where a list of eligible voters is maintained, upon written affirmation by the registrant of the new address on a standard form provided by the registrar at the central location; or

children.label: II
children.text: shall be permitted to correct the voting records for purposes of voting in future elections at the appropriate polling place for the current address and, if permitted by State law, shall be permitted to vote in the present election, upon confirmation by the registrant of the new address by such means as are required by law.

children.label: B
children.text: If State law permits the registrant to vote in the current election upon oral or written affirmation by the registrant of the new address at a polling place described in subparagraph (A)(i) or (A)(ii)(II), voting at the other locations described in subparagraph (A) need not be provided as options.

If the registration records indicate that a registrant has moved from an address in the area covered by a polling place, the registrant shall, upon oral or written affirmation by the registrant before an election official at that polling place that the registrant continues to reside at the address previously made known to the registrar, be permitted to vote at that polling place.

children.heading: Change of voting address within a jurisdiction
children.label: f Change of voting address within a jurisdiction
children.text: In the case of a change of address, for voting purposes, of a registrant to another address within the same registrar’s jurisdiction, the registrar shall correct the voting registration list accordingly, and the registrant’s name may not be removed from the official list of eligible voters by reason of such a change of address except as provided in subsection (d).

On the conviction of a person of a felony in a district court of the United States, the United States attorney shall give written notice of the conviction to the chief State election official designated under of the State of the person’s residence.section 20509 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the name of the offender;

children.label: B
children.text: the offender’s age and residence address;

children.label: C
children.text: the date of entry of the judgment;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of the offenses of which the offender was convicted; and

children.label: E
children.text: the sentence imposed by the court.

On request of the chief State election official of a State or other State official with responsibility for determining the effect that a conviction may have on an offender’s qualification to vote, the United States attorney shall provide such additional information as the United States attorney may have concerning the offender and the offense of which the offender was convicted.

If a conviction of which notice was given pursuant to paragraph (1) is overturned, the United States attorney shall give the official to whom the notice was given written notice of the vacation of the judgment.
The chief State election official shall notify the voter registration officials of the local jurisdiction in which an offender resides of the information received under this subsection.

children.heading: Public disclosure of voter registration activities
children.label: i Public disclosure of voter registration activities

children.text: Each State shall maintain for at least 2 years and shall make available for public inspection and, where available, photocopying at a reasonable cost, all records concerning the implementation of programs and activities conducted for the purpose of ensuring the accuracy and currency of official lists of eligible voters, except to the extent that such records relate to a declination to register to vote or to the identity of a voter registration agency through which any particular voter is registered.

The records maintained pursuant to paragraph (1) shall include lists of the names and addresses of all persons to whom notices described in subsection (d)(2) are sent, and information concerning whether or not each such person has responded to the notice as of the date that inspection of the records is made.
an incorporated city, town, borough, or other form of municipality;

if voter registration is maintained by a county, parish, or other unit of government that governs a larger geographic area than a municipality, the geographic area governed by that unit of government; or

if voter registration is maintained on a consolidated basis for more than one municipality or other unit of government by an office that performs all of the functions of a voting registrar, the geographic area of the consolidated municipalities or other geographic units.
